name: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)
description: Associate-level certification for ML engineers building and deploying ML solutions on AWS. Validates ability to implement ML pipelines, select models, deploy and monitor solutions. Experience with ML on AWS recommended. 85 questions, 170 minutes.
exportIntroQuestions:
## About this document

This document contains practice exam questions related to the **AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)** certification.

### Certification context
This certification validates expertise in:
- Data preparation for ML (ingestion, transformation, feature engineering)
- ML model development (SageMaker, Bedrock)
- Deployment and orchestration of ML workflows
- ML solution monitoring, maintenance, and security

### Purpose
This document compiles practice exam questions for study and review. Each question includes:
- The complete original question statement from the practice exam
- Justification explaining the concept behind the correct answer and the errors in the incorrect alternatives.

### Learning objective
Serve as reference material for discussion and deeper understanding of Machine Learning engineering concepts on AWS.
exportIntroTranscripts:
## About this document

This document contains technical summaries of lesson transcripts related to the **AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)** certification.

### Purpose
Organizes and synthesizes lesson content in a structured format for quick review and deeper understanding of the concepts presented.
exportIntroChat:
## About this document

This document contains AI study conversations related to the **AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate (MLA-C01)** certification.

### Purpose
Records discussions and explanations generated during interactive AI study sessions, serving as complementary reference material.

domains.name: Data Preparation for ML
domains.description: Domain 1 (28%): Ingest and store data for ML workloads, transform and engineer features, ensure data quality and governance for ML.

domains.name: ML Model Development
domains.description: Domain 2 (26%): Choose modeling approach (SageMaker, Bedrock), train and evaluate ML models, select and refine models.

domains.name: Deployment and Orchestration of ML Workflows
domains.description: Domain 3 (22%): Select deployment infrastructure (endpoints, batch), create and script ML pipelines, orchestrate end-to-end workflows.

domains.name: ML Solution Monitoring, Maintenance, and Security
domains.description: Domain 4 (24%): Monitor model performance (drift, bias), maintain ML solutions, secure ML workloads.
